title: Resist adding configuration
body:
You’re going to be under a lot of pressure to make X, Y, and Z, configurable.
Configuration is a perfect example of [tragedy of the commons](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tragedy_of_the_commons).

Everybody wants to add just a tiny piece of configuration to satisfy their use case. As a result we end up with inconsistently designed tools, with unexpected combinations that don’t work correctly, with bugs and regressions that, when fixed in one configuration, break something else with another configuration.

The requests for configuration are vocal. The requests to stay simple are often silent.

I just wanted to take a moment to say there are some of us who actually appreciate tools with limited scope that don’t attempt to solve everybody’s use case, and instead do a specific thing well.

title: Nested ternary formatting - add indents back
body:
Edit: I will no longer be actively participating in this thread - my reasons are explained [in comment below](https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues/5814#issuecomment-719825938).

Hello,

there are many - including me thinking that change to nested ternary is not best decision.

Eg threads https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues/5476 https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues/3018

I know there is some reasoning why nested ternary indent was removed, but it's much harder to read code now.

At least would be good to have option to remain nice formatting (from previous prettier version) - formatting of ternaries with indent and not under each other like:

```
condition
  ? a
  : b
  ? c
  : d
```

instead much easier to understand and read is:

```
condition
  ? a
  : b
    ? c
    : d
```

title: Placing operators at the beginning of lines
body:
<!-- BUGGY OR UGLY? Please use this template.

Tip! Don't write this stuff manually.

1. Go to https://prettier.io/playground
2. Paste your code and set options
3. Press the "Report issue" button in the lower right

-->

**Prettier 1.10.2**
[Playground link](https://prettier.io/playground/#)
```sh
# Options (if any):
--print-width=22
--tab-width=4
```

**Input:**
```js
var a = '123354535435' + '2342423153';
```

**Output:**
```js
var a =
    "123354535435" +
    "2342423153";
```

**Expected behavior:**
```js
var a
    = "123354535435"
    + "2342423153";
```

title: Change HTML/JSX formatting to have one attribute/prop per line
body:
Similar to what was done for #3847 I think it best to break some of the discussion from #3101 into a new issue that people can 👍 or 👎.

I'm proposing that for HTML and JSX to have Prettier always have one attribute/prop per line (and thus not respect the developer's original preference). Code would then be formatted as shown below. This is in contrast to the current behaviour where we fit as much as possible with the print-width.

**Expected behavior:**
```jsx
<MyComponent lorem="1"/>

<MyComponent
  lorem="1"
  ipsum="2"
/>

<MyComponent
  lorem="1"
  ipsum="2"
  dolor="3"
/>
```

This suggestion for one attribute/prop per line was proposed several times in the discussion of #3101 but I think it is clearer if this is pulled into it's own proposal. The original proposal in #3101 is that Prettier would add an option to preserve original formatting which, while I agree with the author with the style that they want, I don't think a) an option, nor b) preserving original formatting follows the aims for Prettier (see also #2068). Instead I think the aims of #3101 are better served by this proposal to ignore the print-width and always place attributes/props on new lines (assuming that there is more than one).

This style appears to be the most common formatting for Angular, Vue and React from what I can tell. It style appears to be the style enforced by the rules:
* React: [jsx-max-props-per-line](https://github.com/yannickcr/eslint-plugin-react/blob/master/docs/rules/jsx-max-props-per-line.md) which [Airbnb set to to 1 in their style guide](https://github.com/airbnb/javascript/blob/master/packages/eslint-config-airbnb/rules/react.js#L89)
* Vue: [max-attributes-per-line](https://github.com/vuejs/eslint-plugin-vue/blob/master/docs/rules/max-attributes-per-line.md) which has a default to 1.

body: This is an amazing tool, any chance at supporting TypeScript as well?

title: Supporting HTML
body: Angular, Vue and Ember all use variants of html/mustache with JS and CSS embedded. It would be really awesome to make prettier support it as well. This would allow the project to be the one stop destination for all the front-end related code.

title: [resolved] [HTML] allow corresponding option to jsxBracketSameLine
body:
Note: Read this comment to make sure you’re up-to-speed on what this issue is and is not about: https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues/5377#issuecomment-628213559

---

For React/JSX - Prettier has [an option](https://prettier.io/docs/en/options.html#jsx-brackets) to allow tags to close on the same line as the declaration, rather then on a new line:
`jsxBracketSameLine: true`:
```
<button
  className="prettier-class"
  id="prettier-id"
  onClick={this.handleClick}>
  Click Here
</button>
```

`jsxBracketSameLine: false`:
```
<button
  className="prettier-class"
  id="prettier-id"
  onClick={this.handleClick}
>
  Click Here
</button>
```
With Prettier 1.15.1, at least for the Angular parser, there is no corresponding option, so all HTML is formatted like this:
Input:
```html
<my-component class="wcd-flex-1 dialog-contents wcd-scroll-vertical"
		      [foo]="bar">
</my-component>
```
Output:
```html
<my-component
    class="wcd-flex-1 dialog-contents wcd-scroll-vertical"
	[foo]="bar"
>
</my-component>
```
[Playground link](https://prettier.io/playground/#)

It would be nice to have a flag to set it such that the output of the above would be:
```html
<my-component
    class="wcd-flex-1 dialog-contents wcd-scroll-vertical"
	[foo]="bar">
</my-component>
```

title: RxJS pipe chaining get formatted on a single line
body:
**Prettier 1.11.1**
[Playground link](https://prettier.io/playground/#N4Igxg9gdgLgprEAuEBLAtgBwgJxgAmHxwEMoBzOfAX3wDMcJ18ByHADwCsBnAeggBG3ODgBuJAQBs4vUhTgsA3AB0oGbHkL50JTABp6qSfBwHuYMjXqNmbLnwiYRJGLm5LVqyFG4FuEAFccMDgAEnwAXmIySgAKAAYDAEZ4gEoVKFV-)

**Input:**
```jsx
import { range } from 'rxjs/observable/range';
import { map, filter, scan } from 'rxjs/operators';

const source$ = range(0, 10);

source$.pipe(
  filter(x => x % 2 === 0),
  map(x => x + x),
  scan((acc, x) => acc + x, 0)
)
.subscribe(x => console.log(x))

```

**Output:**
```jsx
import { range } from "rxjs/observable/range";
import { map, filter, scan } from "rxjs/operators";

const source$ = range(0, 10);

source$
  .pipe(filter(x => x % 2 === 0), map(x => x + x), scan((acc, x) => acc + x, 0))
  .subscribe(x => console.log(x));

```

**Expected behavior:**
As in the [official RxJS documentation](https://github.com/ReactiveX/rxjs/blob/master/doc/pipeable-operators.md), the given input shouldn't be modified as it is considered as methods chaining. The piped functions `filter`, `map` and `scan` should remain on a separate line.
I don't know how it can be handled by prettier without too much impacts, but maybe considering that a function call that includes at least 2 function calls as parameters (eg: `fn1(fn2(), fn3())`) could be printed as a chain as below:
```js
fn1(
  fn2(),
  fn3()
)
```
It would also help when using [Lo-Dash FP chaining](https://medium.com/making-internets/why-using-chain-is-a-mistake-9bc1f80d51ba) which encounters the same problem.

title: Enforce Trailing Commas by Default
body:
I propose that trailing commas should be enabled by default. IE8 is a non issue for most, and this will provide better diffing.

```js
const arr = [
  'multi',
  'line'
]
```

should be formatted as:

```js
const arr = [
  'multi',
  'line',
]
```

I would also suggest not enabling it for function arguments, considering that this is not in the official spec yet and won't be fully supported without a transpiler like babel. e.g.:

```js
foo(
  arg1,
  arg2,
)
```

should be formatted as:

```js
foo(
  arg1,
  arg2
)
```

title: Add option for parentheses around JSX.
Add option to omit semicolons in output
body: E.g. `semicolons: true` to use semicolons at the end of statements (default), and `semicolons: false` to not use semicolons.

title: Linebreaks weirdly in long ES6 template strings with expressions
Feature Request: breakBeforeElse
HTML -> No Django template tags support {% tag %}
Leave one-line functions alone
body:
**Prettier 1.10.2**
[Playground link](https://prettier.io/playground/#)
```sh
--arrow-parens always
```

**Input:**
```jsx
const sha256 = (data) => crypto.createHash("sha256").update(data).digest("hex");
```

**Output:**
```jsx
const sha256 = (data) =>
  crypto
    .createHash("sha256")
    .update(data)
    .digest("hex");

```

**Expected behavior:**

The input was perfectly fine as it stood; it was a nice one-liner, exactly 80 characters long. Breaking it into multiple lines just makes it less clear. Is there a specific reason prettier behaves this way?

title: Tab width should not affect text alignment after the bullet points in Markdown lists
body:
This is a nested list as given in [CommonMark tutorial](https://commonmark.org/help/tutorial/10-nestedLists.html):

```
* Item
    1. First Subitem
    2. Second Subitem
* Item
    - Subitem
    - Subitem
* Item
```

It is formatted like this with `--tab-width=4` (four is a [safer value](https://babelmark.github.io/?text=*+Item%0A++++1.+First+Subitem%0A++++2.+Second+Subitem%0A*+Item%0A++++-+Subitem%0A++++-+Subitem%0A*+Item) than [two](https://babelmark.github.io/?text=*+Item%0A++1.+First+Subitem%0A++2.+Second+Subitem%0A*+Item%0A++-+Subitem%0A++-+Subitem%0A*+Item)):

```
-   Item
    1. First Subitem
    2. Second Subitem
-   Item
    -   Subitem
    -   Subitem
-   Item
```

([Playground](https://prettier.io/playground/#N4Igxg9gdgLgprEAuEAqABASXgWwDpTpHoCMAdOgGICWATgM4zoDKArgEbW4HHoBMFZnEhQAJiw5c4+KBmzSexALQTO3QstVSZc3CAA0ICAAcY1aPWSgAhrVoQA7gAVbCSymsA3CNVEGQ7LTWYADWcDDMxsHUUADmyDC0rHCGMfRwtDBOQbE41sgAZtYANumGAFb0AB4AQkGh4czWOHAAMjFwhSVlIFEMGcggebQhoo5Q-sa0MTAA6r4wABbIABwADIZTEOmzQcaDU3DptJ6dhrRwAI6sdHDZ1rn5SEWlKSDpONQJSW-0MbHFOAARVYEHgXVehhg1nY81ES2QABYoUFqMV-gBhCA4PKDKDQM4gVjpAAqMPcL3SAF8qUA))

I expected this output (just `*` replaced with `-`):

```
- Item
    1. First Subitem
    2. Second Subitem
- Item
    - Subitem
    - Subitem
- Item
```

Reasoning:

- It's the natural form in which nested lists are written.
- I believe it would be less work for Prettier.
- It is similarly easy to look at. There will be personal preferences here but I don't think Prettier generally worries about _beautification_ too much, as long as the code is "correct" (whatever that means in Markdown), reasonably formatted and consistent with common practices.
- It feels illogical to me to apply `--tab-width` both before and after the bullet, also, there is an internal inconsistency when setting tab width greater than 5 as you can see in [`--tab-width=5`](https://prettier.io/playground/#N4Igxg9gdgLgprEAuEAqABASXgWwDpTpHoCMAdOgGICWATgM4zoDKArgEbW4HHoBMFZnEhQAJiw5c4+KBmzSexALQTO3QstVSZc3CAA0ICAAcY1aPWSgAhrVoQA7gAVbCSymsA3CNVEGQ7LTWYADWcDDMxsHUUADmyDC0rHCGMfRwtDBOQbE41sgAZtYANumGAFb0AB4AQkGh4czWOHAAMjFwhSVlIFEMGcggebQhoo5Q-sa0MTAA6r4wABbIABwADIZTEOmzQcaDU3DptJ6dhrRwAI6sdHDZ1rn5SEWlKSDpONQJSW-0MbHFOAARVYEHgXVehhg1nY81ES2QAFYoUFqMV-gBhCA4PKDKDQM4gVjpAAqMPcL3SAF8qUA) vs. [`--tab-width=6`](https://prettier.io/playground/#N4Igxg9gdgLgprEAuEAqABASXgWwDpTpHoCMAdOgGICWATgM4zoDKArgEbW4HHoBMFZnEhQAJiw5c4+KBmzSexALQTO3QstVSZc3CAA0ICAAcY1aPWSgAhrVoQA7gAVbCSymsA3CNVEGQ7LTWYADWcDDMxsHUUADmyDC0rHCGMfRwtDBOQbE41sgAZtYANumGAFb0AB4AQkGh4czWOHAAMjFwhSVlIFEMGcggebQhoo5Q-sa0MTAA6r4wABbIABwADIZTEOmzQcaDU3DptJ6dhrRwAI6sdHDZ1rn5SEWlKSDpONQJSW-0MbHFOAARVYEHgXVehhg1nY81ES2QADYoUFqMV-gBhCA4PKDKDQM4gVjpAAqMPcL3SAF8qUA).
- As a small bonus, the new formatting would be compatible with markdownlint.

I know that nested lists are one of the trickiest parts of Markdown so maybe I'm missing some non-obvious reason why list texts are aligned the way they are.

title: Proposal: Filter using ".gitignore" first, then ".prettierignore" second
body:
- Prettier Version: 2.0.5
- Running Prettier via: CLI
- Runtime: Node.js v12
- Operating System:Windows

**Steps to reproduce:**

From the root of my repo:
```
$ prettier -c .
```

**Expected behavior:**

Prettier should process all files with relevant file extensions, but it should ignore the patterns from `.gitignore`. In other words, ignore the build outputs.  It absolutely does not make sense, by default, to reformat build outputs.

**Actual behavior:**

`prettier -c .` formats source files AND build outputs.

People will tell you to use `.prettierignore` to fix that. But the patterns I find myself writing there are the exact same patterns from `.gitignore`, which may be a complicated file in some repos.

### How did nobody notice this?

From what I can tell (see https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues/6280 for some early research), most Prettier users do not run into this problem. There are a couple reasons why:

1. Nobody uses the Prettier CLI for everyday formatting. Instead they use a wrapper such as `lint-staged` that already applies `.gitignore` filtering.

2. Well hold up -- what about the VS Code extension that reformats files whenever you save?  For that case, people simply don't edit the ignored files very much. 

These factors make the problem less noticeable. However (unless I'm misunderstanding something) the current design is nonetheless a bit misguided.

### Proposed Solution

**Work item 1:** The `--ignore-path` setting needs to be specifiable by `.prettierrc` file.  This setting does not much sense as a CLI parameter. Prettier gets invoked in many ways (`lint-staged`, VS Code extension, Webstorm extension, etc), and all these tools must agree about the `--ignore-path`.

**Work item 2:** The `--ignore-path` setting should be an array.  The files in the array are all considered, and applied one after the other. This allows incremental refinements.

To "opt-in" to the improved behavior, I can now write this:

**.prettierrc.json**
```js
{
  "lineWidth": 100,
  "ignorePath": [ ".gitignore", ".prettierignore" ]
}
```

This way, Prettier will apply `.gitignore` first, then `.prettierignore` only needs to include incremental adjustments.  In most cases `.prettierignore` can be omitted entirely.

**Work item 3:** In some future release, `[ ".gitignore", ".prettierignore" ]` should become the default for the `ignorePath` setting.

title: Disabling "smart formatting" of HTML class attribute
body: _"We get a lot of requests for adding options, but Prettier is built on the principle of being opinionated about code formatting."_ I totally understand this, and so I apologize beforehand for opening this issue. The reason I think this is a valid issue is because the opinion about this seems to have _changed_ from the perspective of Prettier, even though it was mentioned that there's no community interest for this change (and a lot of pushback) in the [introducing pull request](https://github.com/prettier/prettier/pull/7865). **The issue** When using frameworks like [Tailwind](https://github.com/tailwindlabs/tailwindcss), readability has lowered drastically since this feature: **References** - [#836345253](https://github.com/prettier/prettier/pull/7865#issuecomment-836345253) - [#836536543](https://github.com/prettier/prettier/pull/7865#issuecomment-836536543) - [#836652940](https://github.com/prettier/prettier/pull/7865#issuecomment-836652940) - [#839911172](https://github.com/prettier/prettier/pull/7865#issuecomment-839911172) - [#841662012](https://github.com/prettier/prettier/pull/7865#issuecomment-841662012) I'd love to still be able to use Prettier, but this is a blocking issue for me and many others. **The solution** Respecting the `class` attribute to stay on a single line, and only applying formatting it when a user already has a multi-line approach for `class`. Alternatively, an option to disable this feature. Thanks in advance for considering this!

title: Provide way to use single quotes in jsx
body:
I read [this comment](https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues/73#issuecomment-271953751) saying that "I've never seen anyone write `<div className='a' />`". Well, here's someone 👋 

The same way `trailingComma` accepts a string, we could let `singleQuote` do as well. 
- `'all'` would use single quotes in js as well as jsx
- `'js'` (or `true`) would use single quotes in js but not in jsx
- `'jsx'` would use single quotes in jsx but not in js

title: Support for the new Angular Control Flow Syntax
body:
Angular is introducing a new control flow syntax in it's [RFC](https://github.com/angular/angular/discussions/50719).

I'm opening this issue in order to track the support for it in the prettier formatter.

Because the new sytanx follows the svelte syntax a lot, I believe we can learn/be-inspired/copy a lot from the svelte one.

title: JSX {" "} spaces
body:
Is there any way at all to stop Prettier adding these horribly ugly spaces? Perhaps just _not_ format strings like this if it needs to add spaces like this. **Prettier 1.11.1** [Playground link](https://prettier.io/playground/#) **Input:** ```jsx

hello LINK world

``` **Output:** ```jsx

hello{" "} LINK {" "} world

; ```

title: Prettier 2.0 (old)
body:
**Note added by @j-f1:**

### This is NOT the current plan for Prettier v2.0. We’ve significantly scaled back our plans for what Prettier 2.0 will do, allowing us to get a less-controversial release out sooner: https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues/6888

---

I've been thinking a bit about what we would do **if** (don't create a milestone just yet :wink:) we were to do a Prettier 2.0 release with some API/CLI breaking changes. No significant changes to code formatting other than perhaps changing some defaults.

### Easy?

* #68/#3469 - Change default value for `trailingComma` to `"es5"`.
  The diff advantages alone are excellent, and this seems to be commonplace among the most popular JS projects.

### CLI:

* #2691 - CLI defaults to `"**/*.{js,ts,...}"`.
  People should just be able to do `prettier --write` without passing messy globs _#0CJS_

* #2846 - Replace the "parser" option with "language".
  e.g. `echo ".foo {}" | prettier --language css`
  Keep `--parser flow` or perhaps support something like `--parser.js flow` for future extensibility. 

* Remove `--flow-parser` option.
  Has been deprecated for a long time.

### For debate

- #838 - Consistently add quotes to object keys.
  e.g. put quotes around `origin` in this example:
  ```js
  const headers = {
    'origin': 'foo.com',
    'access-control-allow-origin': '*',
  };
  ```

- Change the default value for `singleQuote` to `true`
  * Airbnb, standard, xo, and probably the majority of JS devs prefer this. Maybe do a Twitter poll to gauge opinions on this.

> To be clear, the Airbnb config requires single quotes for JS and double quotes for JSX.

### Outdated

* <del>#2884 - Remove the default parser.</del> <ins>Fixed by  #4528</ins>

* ~#1139 - Put a space after the `n` in `function () {}`~

title: Support TypeScript 3.8
body:
[TypeScript 3.8 beta has been released!🎉🎉](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/typescript/announcing-typescript-3-8-beta/)
We should support some syntax will be add via TS 3.8.

There are some syntaxes that can support by just (waiting and) updating typescript-estree(e.g.  [Top Level await](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/typescript/announcing-typescript-3-8-beta/#top-level-await)), but there seems to be some that need to be supported on the Prettier side.(e.g. [Type only imports exports](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/typescript/announcing-typescript-3-8-beta/#type-only-imports-exports))

New syntax:
- `import type`, `export type`
- ECMAScript Private Fields
- `export * as ns`

title: Disable prettier for a single file
body:
Hi! Thank you so much for this wonderful project, it's been a lifesaver and is one of my favorite tools of all time. 

When contributing to other people's projects, there are times when I would like to disable prettier to not reformat everything on save. I love my own rules but every project I contribute to is a little different. I'd love to not have to disable the whole thing just to contribute to something else. What do you think? 

Apologies if someone has asked for this before, I searched open issues and couldn't find anything addressing it.

_update by @thorn0:_ this issue was reopened to track `@noformat`/ `@noprettier`: https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues/3634#issuecomment-364297344

title: Multiple files for --ignore-path
body:
I use the [--ignore-path](https://prettier.io/docs/en/cli.html#--ignore-path) argument and point it to an exhaustive `.gitignore` file. It's great that I don't have to duplicate the list of ignored files.

However, there are a couple of files I'd like to exclude from prettifying but still want to commit. Is there any way to specify an additonal `.prettierignore` file so that I can add files without copying the entire `.gitignore`?

title: Indent script and style tags content in *.vue files
Prettier 2.0 – 2.0 2020
body:
We already have a Prettier 2.0 issue – #3503 – but tell you what: Time’s up for that one. As of this writing, Node.js 8 is scheduled to expire early on December 31, 2019. And we still support Node.js 4. In two months we will be 6 versions behind!

Supporting Node.js 4 and Node.js 6 has been a real hassle lately as our dependencies (rightfully!) has stopped supporting them. We have spent a lot of unnecessary time getting tests to pass in Node.js 4.

So here’s my suggestion for a 2.0 of a Prettier 2.0 issue:

**The main focus should be dropping support for unsupported Node.js versions.** Let’s aim for releasing Prettier 2.0 early in January 2020 with support for only Node.js 10+.

Apart from that we could do some easy breaking changes:

- Change the default for `trailingComma` to `es5`, as discussed in the original Prettier 2.0 thread.
- Remove deprecated stuff.

When that is done – if we have time, we could do more things. We’ll see. For example, it would be really nice addressing #5588 (making all unsafe template literal formatting **opt-in**). I also have a suggestion for lots of improvements for Prettier’s CLI and API, but I’ll open a separate issue for that.

/cc @prettier/core @brodybits @fisker

title: Configuration file support
body:
I think it would be valuable to support for some type of file that contains prettier's config so that projects can check it in source control.

I can see 2 cases where this would be useful.
- Allow tools to know that the project is using prettier. I'd like to enable thing like format on save but I don't want to reformat everyfile I touch on a project that doesn't use prettier.
- Allow per project config to avoid the need to change options when changing projects or to have to create custom formating scripts.

Could be it's own file (something like `.editorconfig`) or maybe part of `package.json`.

title: Markdown Unordered List Item Symbol
body:
Hello Prettier! I'm a big fan of using Prettier in my JavaScript projects, and I recommend it to all my friends an colleagues. I was really excited about the addition of Markdown formatting, until I found that unordered list items are delineated with `*` instead of `-` (see [this issue](https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues/3025) for initial discussion).

I have never even seen `*` used for list items before Prettier started changing my markdown files. I think I knew it was part of the Markdown spec but I'd never seen it used in practice. I stopped using Prettier for Markdown as a result, and [I'm not the only one](https://github.com/strongloop/loopback-next/pull/1139). I do appreciate that Prettier is opinionated, but in this case the "opinion" was informed only by a measure of "what's more common" based on a [Big Data query](https://bigquery.cloud.google.com/savedquery/652929483875:69be015faec8444c8a2e7d055eeb32f8) that I can't seem to access (so I can't assess the quality of the data). I would argue that, absent an advocate from either side, this opinion lacks context. And even taking the data on face value, `-` use stood at 39%, which I believe is enough to warrant a discussion around an option to support it. 

Personally, `-` makes far more sense to me as a list item symbol because it matches the YAML notation for list items. It is also widely used in word processors to begin lists (type `- ` on a new line and both Word and Pages will automatically convert it into a list for you). Not so with `*`. 

A [popular Markdown style guide](http://www.cirosantilli.com/markdown-style-guide/#option-list-marker-hyphen) recommends `-` for list items because `*` can be confused for bold/italic markers. [Other style guides](https://github.com/carwin/markdown-styleguide) also recommend `-` (although I did [find one guide](https://github.com/google/styleguide/blob/gh-pages/docguide/style.md#lists) that _used_ `*` but did not explicitly recommending it).

Based on discussion [on the initial issue](https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues/3025), there is strong interest in an option for `-`, and even interest in outright changing to `-` entirely. What do others think? Thanks for your consideration.

title: Angular Output/Input decorators are moved to new line
Semicolon option
body:
**Many modern languages don't force us to type "semicolon / ;" at the end of each line.**
I was so happy to know that JavaScript also supports that feature.
After switched to no semicolon style, I don't want to go back anymore.
Of course, there is some special case that we need a semicolon.
Luckily, VS Code is smart enough to warn me those edge cases.

Therefore, I believe **a sophisticated prettier should have an option for "no semicolon-ers".**
It should "off / disabled" by default. (I know everyone still loves semicolons)

title: Option for ignoring original formatting 100%
body:
Edit by @lydell: Prettier currently keeps multiline object literals multiline even if they fit in a single line, and does special things to blank lines: removes some, collapses consecutive blank lines into one and never adds blank lines. This is because the majority of people seem to want this level of control since we haven’t found a super good heuristic for printing those things. Some people prefer a 100% uniform printing, though, that _completely_ ignores original formatting, even if it sometimes results in “uglier” code. We should discuss this.

Original post:

Is it possible to have a strict option that disambiguates whitespace and always prints out the same so long as the AST is the same? I'm running into some weird issues where lines wrap and then don't unwrap when they are under the line-length limit. This would greatly help with some of my merge conflicts right now :)

P.S. I'm using typescript.

Thanks

title: Parentheses around single parameter arrow functions / Add support for arrow-parens property
Prefer escaping single quotes in strings rather than mixing quote styles.
body:
Currently, this string (`'\'editing\' flag'`) is automatically converted to `"'editing' flag"` - is there a way to prefer escaped single quotes? The value of this preference is that it promotes a consistent code style.

In my opinion, there should be an option to leave the single quotes alone. What do you guys think?

Edit: I've encountered this issue in my Jest tests and I have the --single-quote option set.

title: Consider respecting EditorConfig file for whitespace and encoding options
body: This would allow configuration of line breaks and tab characters, at least: http://editorconfig.org

title: Support golang template/html
body:
We should consider supporting golang's template/html syntax. This syntax is gaining popularity and is being used by many people, especially because of static site generators like [Hugo](https://gohugo.io/). Prettier works fine for certain things already but breaks on others). In some cases prettier formats as expected but sometimes it completely breaks the code. A good implementation should fix this. I am including some examples below.

### Correct
Basic if statement: [Playground link](https://prettier.io/playground/#N4Igxg9gdgLgprEAuEAeAhAEQPIGEAqAmgAoCiABABYwC2ANgHwA6Uq19zU55qARhABMAnp27dUAgJYA3TkxjzgwcpIBm5OAEdyAOigBDGnHJMQAGQCuYfafIBfOyzFj58gBJw6dCOUvWnziYwSggCDgHiAPRSsgGokfzCnPHsjCAANCAQAA4wktAAzsig+gBOpRAA7sRlCEUo+tIQkgIZILyl+mAA1nAwAMrZXZJQAObIMKUWcJmpAOqUkvAFQ2Bw-XVLMktCyOAFRZkjBXClMMSdozT6yKr6dCeZAFYFAB4AQp09ff2GcGYjOC3e6PEAvV79EajOhwACKFgg8GBDxmICGpROpT2qTa2VKIxgcxaMEoyAAHAAGTJ4iAnOadbJ7PFwTHSIGZUpaCySTkXfRXG5IO4ozInGiSCZTVEFKEw+GIoFCkGomD6XhEgQk5AAJkyk30kjoUNwEBo1z2UGg7JAFhO+DV9WFJwcQA)
Basic ranging: [Playground link](https://prettier.io/playground/#)

### Not correct
Basic template: [Playground link](https://prettier.io/playground/#N4Igxg9gdgLgprEAuExgAIAmcBmBLKOdAHRAAcBDAczlPQF97ioAVACzwGd0v0Ko+AJxh4wAGzgA6dAB4ARoPQB6AHzM06aY3UYEmBkyggANCAhkR0TslAVBgiAHcACnYTWUFAG4Q8mEyAKFGAA1nAwAMqUYARUyDCCAK5wpmwwALZiAOoc8JzRcBHueCJeJQCeyOCc1qYEnHDCzoLU6RTIOBRiDaYAVpwAHgBCLaHhERTpcAAyBHAdXT0g-QMRsRIAiokQ8AvdKeR2DYJVaZkBZIIEMFl+MGzIABwADKaXEA1ZLWRVl3DHXnmpkEcAAjok8CDmq12khOvtTA10nh4kkDpx1nAtjt5nDFgcYBQ5LdMPdkAAmUwJCh4MSxADCEHSbSqUGgQJAiQaLCJHnhDUYQA)
Another basic template: [Playground link](https://prettier.io/playground/#N4Igxg9gdgLgprEAuExgAIAmcBmBLKOdAHRAAcBDAczlPQF97ioAVACzwGd0v0Ko+AJxh4wAGzgA6dADkKAWzhJ0adJIrDREyVAVFGzVQkwN6IADQgIZEdE7JQGwRADuABQ0J7KCgDcIeJgWIABGghRgANZwMADKlGAEVMgwggCucJZsMPJiAOoc8JwJcLFeeCK+FQCeyOCc9pYEnHDCbuFU8hTIOBRiLZYAVpwAHgBC4VExsXoAMgRwPX0DIMMjsUkSAIppEPBL-ZnkGi2Cddm5wWSCBDB5gTBsyAAcAAyW1xAteeFkdddwU6+RaWQRwACOaTwYPa1C6BxWLXkeBS6SOnE2cB2e0WSF6h0sMAoIXumEeyAATITwngxEkAMIQeTwlBQaAgkBpFosYnefEtRhAA)

title: Release 1.19 checklist
body: - prepare - [x] Complete [milestone items](https://github.com/prettier/prettier/milestone/32) - [x] Run on other projects to check for regressions - [x] Write release notes - #6787 add a Markdown file named [2019-11-03-1.19.0.md](https://github.com/prettier/prettier/blob/master/website/blog/2019-09-16-1.19.0.md) in [`website/blog`](https://github.com/prettier/prettier/tree/master/website/blog) - [prs-merged-since](https://npm.im/prs-merged-since) may be useful - publish - [x] Run [release script](https://github.com/prettier/prettier/tree/master/scripts/release) - postpublish - [x] Adjust release notes date - [x] Merge release notes - [x] Wait for release notes to deploy - [x] Tweet from [`@PrettierCode`](https://twitter.com/PrettierCode) account via TweetDeck - [x] Close milestone - [x] Send schema PR generated by `scripts/generate-schema.js` to [SchemaStore](https://github.com/SchemaStore/schemastore/blob/master/src/schemas/json/prettierrc.json) if we added a new option. - [x] Update the [release checklist](https://github.com/prettier/prettier/wiki/Release-Checklist) with possible changes – opened #6887 - [x] Close #3888 Then monitor [Twitter](https://twitter.com/search?q=%40PrettierCode&src=typed_query&f=live)/[GitHub](https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues) for any immediate issues 😄

title: Keep comments that are near an `if`’s condition closer to that condition
body:
<details>

<summary>The original Prettier 1.13.6 Playground link</summary>

**Prettier 1.13.6**
[Playground link](https://prettier.io/playground/#)
```sh
--parser babylon
--trailing-comma false
```

---

</details>

**Prettier 2.1.2**
[Playground link](https://prettier.io/playground/#-kkAFbcDgAIWabQ6nToNDgM2kcHBkJGIFhHE6diqcAAioNWNikBCqlCQD1cv9crsGnQJlVjilctIYBdUMIYBJkAAOAAMSS5EH+F2aKV2XLgTJe2KSAEcKfBgakKntuABaKBwSaTY65ODq1Bm4FLMHU3FJf40VD9IZ47jEskaqk0ukwNl8gVCpAAJiSAzoqCqdgAwhAaLaQIqAKzHQb-MRsio+vEvYYASSg01gnTA3LSAEFC50YAwSTjaXAVCogA)
```sh
--parser babel
```

**Input:**
```jsx
if (foo) { // foo may not exist
  doThing(foo);
}

if (foo)
// foo may not exist
{
  doThing(foo);
}

if (foo) /* foo may not exist */ {
  doThing(foo);
}
```

**Output:**
```jsx
if (foo) {
  // foo may not exist
  doThing(foo);
}

if (foo) {
  // foo may not exist
  doThing(foo);
}

if (foo) {
  /* foo may not exist */ doThing(foo);
}

```

In the current output, the comments are moved into the block after the `if`.

As for the desired output, I'm not sure in each of the three cases where the comments should go. Either they **shouldn't be moved**:

```javascript
if (foo) { // foo may not exist
  doThing(foo);
}

if (foo)
// foo may not exist
{
  doThing(foo);
}

if (foo) /* foo may not exist */ {
  doThing(foo);
}
```

Or they should be **moved *before* the `if`**, rather than into the block after the `if`:

```javascript
// foo may not exist
if (foo) {
  doThing(foo);
}

// foo may not exist
if (foo) {
  doThing(foo);
}

/* foo may not exist */ if (foo) {
  doThing(foo);
}
```

I think the second case has a higher likelihood of deserving to be moved than the first and third cases.

### Related issue

Pull request #672 “Stabilize comments inside of if/then/else before {” made a change related to the last of the three cases, but it simply made the current output stable, rather than changing what that output was.

### Similar cases with acceptable output

For completeness, these similar cases are formatted in a way I am okay with, though it’s possible others would want the comment placement to always be preserved:

<det

title: Support Handlebars
body: I am opening this issue to track Handlebars support. It was previously tracked in https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues/1882 but that was closed when we added HTML support, so this issue is for Handlebars only.

title: No line breaks for very long arrays?
body:
Just wanted to kick off a discussion about how Prettier prints long arrays. 😊 I think in some cases it might make sense to have Prettier format a little differently.

Let's take the following as an example:

```js
const userIds = [
  941,
  722,
  312,
  933,
  841,
  166,
  843,
  978,
  436,
  687,
  269,
  573,
  913,
  873,
  748,
  354,
  153,
  181,
  947,
  563,
  324,
  952,
  885,
  911,
  698,
  248,
  764,
  431,
  811,
  344,
  855,
  430,
  396,
  981,
  594,
  131,
  489,
  552,
  116,
  833,
  521,
  252,
  503,
  540,
  893,
  736,
  969,
  145,
  925,
  417,
  344,
  108,
  161,
  777,
  971,
  159,
  158,
  908,
  511,
  876,
  768,
  284,
  640,
  309,
  651,
  292,
  898,
  284,
  201,
  506,
  654,
  840,
  334,
  246,
  376,
  398,
  714,
  134,
  435,
  181,
  980,
  594,
  396,
  100,
  743,
  812,
  583,
  622,
  800,
  310,
  111,
  537,
  751,
  920,
  872,
  700,
  702,
  655,
  515,
  298
];
```

Lately, I find myself inserting `// prettier-ignore` above arrays like this and adjusting the formatting manually to something like this:

```js
// prettier-ignore
const userIds = [
  941, 722, 312, 933, 841, 166, 843, 978, 436, 687, 269, 573, 913, 873, 748,
  354, 153, 181, 947, 563, 324, 952, 885, 911, 698, 248, 764, 431, 811, 344,
  855, 430, 396, 981, 594, 131, 489, 552, 116, 833, 521, 252, 503, 540, 893,
  736, 969, 145, 925, 417, 344, 108, 161, 777, 971, 159, 158, 908, 511, 876,
  768, 284, 640, 309, 651, 292, 898, 284, 201, 506, 654, 840, 334, 246, 376,
  398, 714, 134, 435, 181, 980, 594, 396, 100, 743, 812, 583, 622, 800, 310,
  111, 537, 751, 920, 872, 700, 702, 655, 515, 298
];
```

I think that in cases like this, it doesn't really help to have each item on a new line. It just makes the code harder to scroll through and harder to read. Line breaks definitely make sense for shorter arrays, but there could be some sort of a thresh-hold after which we could format differently.

What do you think?

title: Space after function keyword - MOVED to #3847!
Add cache option to avoid processing unchanged files
body:
<!--

BEFORE SUBMITTING AN ISSUE:

1.  Search for your issue on GitHub: https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues
    A large number of opened issues are duplicates of existing issues.
    If someone has already opened an issue for what you are experiencing,
    you do not need to open a new issue — please add a 👍 reaction to the
    existing issue instead.

2.  If your issue is with a prettier editor extension or add-on, please open the
    issue in the repo for that extension or add-on, instead of this repo.

-->

**Environments:**
- Prettier Version: 1.16.4
- Running Prettier via: CLI (package.json script)<!-- CLI, Node.js API, Browser API, etc. -->
- Runtime: Node.js 8.11.3 <!-- Node.js v6, Chrome v67, etc. -->
- Operating System: MacOS 10.13.6<!-- Windows, Linux, macOS, etc. -->

**Steps to reproduce:**
<!-- shell script, js code, or a link to the minimal reproducible repository -->
N/A

**Expected behavior:**
That when provided with an option such as `--cache`, prettier only process new or changed files. See [eslint](https://eslint.org/docs/user-guide/command-line-interface#--cache) for example.

**Actual behavior:**
Prettier processes all files, whether they have changed or not.

title: JSDoc trailing whitespaces are deleted
body: Two trailing whitespace in a JSDoc are used to indicate a line break. Prettier keeps deleting them for me. Is there a way to configure it to ignore trailing whitespaces in JSDoc, like in the tslint (`"no-trailing-whitespace": [true, "ignore-jsdoc"]`)?

title: TypeScript constructor assignment improvement.
Support setext headings in Markdown
body:
## Prettier 1.16.4
[Playground link](https://prettier.io/playground/#)
```json5
{
	"parser": "markdown",
	"tabWidth": 4,
	"useTabs": true,

	/**
	 * `markdownHeadings` values:
	 * - `atx` (default)
	 * - `setext`
	 * - `setext-h1` (only `<h1>` uses the setext syntax)
	 */
	"markdownHeadings": "setext",
}
```

### Input:
````markdown
Setext Heading 1
================

Setext Heading 2
----------------

## ATX Heading 2

````

### Output:
````markdown
# Setext Heading 1

## Setext Heading 2

## ATX Heading 2

````

## Expected behavior:
Setext headers are used:
````markdown
Setext Heading 1
================

Setext Heading 2
----------------

ATX Heading 2
-------------

````

Preferably, I’d also like if there was an option to have only Heading 1 use the setext syntax, and the rest use the ATX syntax.

title: Inconsistency breaking assignments
body:
For example,

```js
aParticularlyLongAndObnoxiousNameForIllustrativePurposes = 
  anotherVeryLongNameForIllustrativePurposes;
```

[formats as](https://prettier.io/playground/#%7B%22content%22%3A%22aParticularlyLongAndObnoxiousNameForIllustrativePurposes%20%3D%20%5Cn%20%20anotherVeryLongNameForIllustrativePurposes%3B%5Cn%22%2C%22options%22%3A%7B%22printWidth%22%3A80%2C%22tabWidth%22%3A2%2C%22singleQuote%22%3Afalse%2C%22trailingComma%22%3A%22none%22%2C%22bracketSpacing%22%3Atrue%2C%22jsxBracketSameLine%22%3Afalse%2C%22parser%22%3A%22babylon%22%2C%22semi%22%3Atrue%2C%22useTabs%22%3Afalse%2C%22doc%22%3Afalse%2C%22ast%22%3Afalse%7D%7D)

```js
aParticularlyLongAndObnoxiousNameForIllustrativePurposes = anotherVeryLongNameForIllustrativePurposes;
```

Similarly there is no break after the `=` in

```js
aParticularlyLongAndObnoxiousNameForIllustrativePurposes =
  'a very long string for illustrative purposes'.length;
```
or
```js
aParticularlyLongAndObnoxiousNameForIllustrativePurposes =
  anotherVeryLongNameForIllustrativePurposes();
```
although there *is* in
```js
aParticularlyLongAndObnoxiousNameForIllustrativePurposes =
  anotherVeryLongNameForIllustrativePurposes.length;
```
and
```js
aParticularlyLongAndObnoxiousNameForIllustrativePurposes =
  anotherVeryLongNameForIllustrativePurposes + 1;
```

I'd expect every one of these to break after the `=`, since otherwise line length exceeds 80 characters. (Though I thought it used to... is this an intentional, or a regression?)

[Here's a link](https://prettier.io/playground/#%7B%22content%22%3A%22aParticularlyLongAndObnoxiousNameForIllustrativePurposes%20%3D%20%5Cn%20%20anotherVeryLongNameForIllustrativePurposes%3B%5Cn%5CnaParticularlyLongAndObnoxiousNameForIllustrativePurposes%20%3D%5Cn%20%20'a%20very%20long%20string%20for%20illustrative%20purposes'.length%3B%5Cn%5CnaParticularlyLongAndObnoxiousNameForIllustrativePurposes%20%3D%5Cn%20%20anotherVeryLongNameForIllustrativePurposes.length%3B%5Cn%5CnaParticularlyLongAndObnoxiousNameForIllustrativePurposes%20%3D%5Cn%20%20anotherVeryLongNameForIllustrativePurposes()%3B%5Cn%5CnaParticularlyLongAndObnoxiousNameForIllustrativePurposes%20%3D%5Cn%20%20anotherVeryLongNameForIllustrativePurposes%20%2B%201%3B%5Cn%22%2C%22options%22%3A%7B%22printWidth%22%3A80%2C%22tabWidth%22%3A2%2C%22singleQuote%22%3Afalse%2C%22trailingComma%22%3A%22none%22%2C%22bracketSpacing%22%3Atrue%2C%22jsxBracketSameLine%22%3Afalse%2C%22parser%22%3A%22babylon%22%2C%22semi%22%3Atrue%2C%22useTabs%22%3Afalse%2C%22doc%22%3Afalse%2C%22ast%22%3Afalse%7D%7D) containing all of these cases.

This also applies to variable declarations.

title: Making parser or printer async
body:
This is a feature request, which may be useful for plugins that delegate parsing or compiling to an external processes. I can recall at least `prettier-plugin-ruby` (see [its parser.js](https://github.com/iamsolankiamit/prettier-ruby/blob/04de15a45869ce6ea7cfaac29ecda34ab15ccef7/src/parser.js#L7-L11)) and `prettier-plugin-elm` ([parser.js](https://github.com/gicentre/prettier-plugin-elm/blob/1a15233d580a68d49dbf1ab4455863e0f9cc74dc/src/parser.js#L6-L11)) that do this.

At the moment, when Prettier is given a markdown file, the code blocks in it are parsed and printed sequentially, which means that each third-party process is spawned one after another. This can quickly slow things down and make _Format on save_ feel quite glitchy (2-3 seconds of wait time when there are 10-20 code blocks is a bit too much). It would be great if the plugins' `parse()` or `print()` methods could return promises and thus allow Prettier to process more than one fenced code block in parallel (e.g. in a queue).

How feasible this internal change would be? What are the downsides? Is there any possible gain in calling standard JavaScript parsers and printers asynchronously too (e.g. to do block parsing in workers)?

Curious to know what you guys think!

title: YAML: long property names/keys get prefixed with question mark `?`, and colon `:` gets wrapped onto the next line
body:
**Prettier 1.15.3**
[Playground link](https://prettier.io/playground/#)
```sh
--parser yaml
```

**Input:**
```yaml
very_long_name_in_fact_it_is_longer_than_80_characters_123123123123123123123123123:
  foo: "bar"
```

**Output:**
```yaml
? very_long_name_in_fact_it_is_longer_than_80_characters_123123123123123123123123123
: foo: "bar"

```

**Expected behavior:**

Doesn't wrap the `:` to the second line, i.e. leaves the whole thing intact.

```yaml
very_long_name_in_fact_it_is_longer_than_80_characters_123123123123123123123123123:
  foo: "bar"
```

This issue looks simple to fix, where would I look for the related code if I wanted to make a PR?

title: [Windows] Error with file name containing square brackets and dots when running 'prettier --write'
body:
<!--

BEFORE SUBMITTING AN ISSUE:

1.  Search for your issue on GitHub: https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues
    A large number of opened issues are duplicates of existing issues.
    If someone has already opened an issue for what you are experiencing,
    you do not need to open a new issue — please add a 👍 reaction to the
    existing issue instead.

2.  If your issue is with a Prettier editor extension or add-on, please open the
    issue in the repo for that extension or add-on, instead of this repo.

-->

**Environments:**

- Prettier Version: 3.0.3
- Running Prettier via: CLI
- Runtime: Node.js v18.15 
- Operating System:  Windows
- Prettier plugins (if any): No

**Steps to reproduce:**

1- Create a file with the name [...nextauth].ts at the root of your project (mine is a nextjs project).
2- Run the command prettier --write with this file as an argument.

**Expected behavior:**
I expected that running the 'prettier --write' command with a file named '[...nextauth].ts'  would execute without errors, formatting the file as intended.

**Actual behavior:**
 Observe the following error: "Explicitly specified file was ignored due to negative glob patterns."

title: MDX: support Markdown inside JSX blocks
body:
According to the [commonmark spec](https://spec.commonmark.org/0.29/#example-136), it seems that the example below is valid MDX. However, Prettier is throwing an error.

**Prettier 1.19.1**
[Playground link](https://prettier.io/playground/#N4Igxg9gdgLgprEAuEAeAwhAJnAygVwCcAzOQgPgB0pqADegKwGdrIomIAbOAOk4gDmACgCMASgDcdetWqoA9JhwESZciAA0ICAAcYAS2hNkoAIaFCEAO4AFcwmMpTANwj6smkACNCpsAGs4GFwdP30oAWQYQnw4LQALGABbTgB1eP14JlCwPAdM-WdMgE9kcCZjLXCmMhgbXwEk02RiU04arWYADwAhXwCg3FMkuAAZcLgWto6QbtxwgW4ARXwIeCn2uJBQwhrCMqSsLs8dQnCYVPcYeOQADgAGLVOIGtTfHTLTuD3nSa0AR1W8HqukcIFMTAAtFA4HAcB4tIQ4ID9Ej6qZGs0kK1Nloakl9FEYlsmAtlkDJtjplsYKYvJcsNdkAAmLTRUz6TgLTBJJplKDQP4gfA1AAqdMcOJqAF9pUA)
```sh
--parser mdx
```

**Input:**
````mdx
<CodeSurfer>

```js
console.log(1);
```

</CodeSurfer>
````

**Output:**
````mdx
SyntaxError: Unexpected closing tag "CodeSurfer". It may happen when the tag has already been closed by another tag. For more info see https://www.w3.org/TR/html5/syntax.html#closing-elements-that-have-implied-end-tags (1:1)
> 1 | <CodeSurfer>
    | ^
  2 | 
  3 | ```js
  4 | console.log(1);
````

**Expected behavior:**

If this is valid MDX it should parse it and format it without an error.

title: Parentheses around nullish coalescing operator within conditional expression
body:
**Prettier 1.13.5**
[Playground link](https://prettier.io/playground/#-ACKhAg8E6JU+IBgvDmVEWyAATGZkrgaPgfgBhCB5AreDRQE4gQgZAAqvC8jy6cAAvhSgA)
```sh
--parser babylon
```

**Input:**
```jsx
const value = condition ? (nullableValue ?? fallbackValue) : nonNullableValue;
```

**Output:**
```jsx
const value = condition ? nullableValue ?? fallbackValue : nonNullableValue;

```

**Expected behavior:**

I think it would be better if Prettier preserved the parentheses around the arguments of the nullish coalescing operator if nested within a conditional expression:

```jsx
const value = condition ? (nullableValue ?? fallbackValue) : nonNullableValue;
```

My concern is that the precedence of `??` isn't immediately obvious, and I think that parentheses would improve readability here.

title: Add parentheses around `!...` on the left of `in`/`instanceof`
body:
<!--

BEFORE SUBMITTING AN ISSUE:

1.  Search for your issue on GitHub: https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues
    A large number of opened issues are duplicates of existing issues.
    If someone has already opened an issue for what you are experiencing,
    you do not need to open a new issue — please add a 👍 reaction to the
    existing issue instead.

2.  We get a lot of requests for adding options, but Prettier is
    built on the principle of being opinionated about code formatting.
    This means we add options only in the case of strict technical necessity.
    Find out more: https://prettier.io/docs/en/option-philosophy.html

Don't fill the form below manually! Let a program create a report for you:

1. Go to https://prettier.io/playground
2. Paste your code and set options
3. Press the "Report issue" button in the lower right

-->

**Prettier 3.0.3**
[Playground link](https://prettier.io/playground/#)

```sh
# Options (if any):
```

**Input:**

```jsx
if (!x in y) {}
if (!x instanceof y) {}

if ((!x) in y) {}
if ((!x) instanceof y) {}
```

**Output:**

```jsx
if (!x in y) {
}
if (!x instanceof y) {
}

if (!x in y) {
}
if (!x instanceof y) {
}
```

**Expected behavior:**

```jsx
if ((!x) in y) {
}
if ((!x) instanceof y) {
}

if ((!x) in y) {
}
if ((!x) instanceof y) {
}
```

**Motivation**

At a [TC39 meeting next week](https://github.com/tc39/agendas/blob/main/2023/09.md) we'll discuss about a proposal for `!in` and `!instanceof`, to allow writing `x !in y` rather than `!(x in y)`.

One of the main motivator factors is that it's very common ([see the slides that will be presented](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1vwNOjUiUvy6TzK6t0Mb8qgyKwBNszly9HibJJpUa_Eo/edit#slide=id.g27f462e9ca3_0_0)) to forget parentheses around `(x in y)` when writing `!(x in y)`.

I wonder if prettier could format `!x in y` as `(!x) in y`, similarly to how it formats `a ^ b | c` to `(a ^ b) | c`, so that developers can more easily notice that their code is wrong.

title: Feature request: Range ignore in CSS
body:
Originally submitted as https://github.com/prettier/prettier-vscode/issues/1023

Related:
- https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues/5557
- https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues/5508
- https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues/5008

(Wonder if there should be a master tracking issue for adding the ranged ignore to all supported file formats?)

### Summary

Prettier has a feature for ranged ignores  <https://prettier.io/docs/en/ignore.html#range-ignore> since v1.12.0+. This does not work in CSS (and any other format than Markdown, for that matter).


[Prettier Playground](https://prettier.io/playground/#-WBIJULARb1mYAWyVS6QAHBTvEDeGCoIwxBFDKd3vFIGjya4sjl8ji6cIhAhmdUiR8AG6c5Y8vK0rBgoRC2AANQJL1Zs0kUoxuSY3QAjAb+VgACJSUZK8pESrqxFsiziRpk5ZdQFeY2YE0NJgAFVk2FMtrF8XEksWXMxOU2HpNcDAqpsQZmllI2vS7hpHoAorhBLhCcn4nBlHYrlnFPAGdbniy7bM+DZw8s1lG5ZgYRAGgWkXxRmm-hluhTWnAPVDRkYKjXRYWLEQRKXgQ8hNhaCLXrOiKSfukZXzCrwnnxaEIGtXu-aANbwpsY-49NsAaTgslhtHEF9mEGw-cyGX---j+EAA)

### Expected result

prettier does not reformat the section defined with `prettier-ignore-start` and `prettier-ignore-end`

### Actual result

prettier reformats all the lines.

### Additional information

```
prettier --version
1.19.1
```

prettier-vscode 2.3.0

![prettier-vscode](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2943750/68535687-fa4e7680-034e-11ea-8e8e-91ba0d1fcf56.gif)

title: Can't use Prettier within a browser
body:
Following up from [this Twitter comment](https://twitter.com/suchipi/status/932517379367608320); relates to babel/website/issues/1388 and babel/website/pull/1466.

The Babel REPL has been using Prettier via [bundle.run](https://bundle.run/) for some time. Version 1.7.0 broke our ability to do this with the error: "_cosmiconfig requires at least version 4 of Node_". Version 1.8.0 introduced a different error: "_os$2.homedir is not a function_".

It would be ideal if Prettier output a browser-friendly UMD bundle for use-cases like this, but in lieu of that maybe it could at least work via a solution like rundle.run? 😄 

For the time being, we've pinned Babel to use Prettier version 1.6.1. You can try it out [here](https://deploy-preview-1466--babel.netlify.com/repl/#?babili=false&browsers=&build=&builtIns=false&code_lz=MYewdgzgLgBAhjAvDA3gKBjARgLhgcmHwBoMYATPAIgFMrSBfIA&debug=false&circleciRepo=&evaluate=false&lineWrap=false&presets=react%2Cstage-2&prettier=true&targets=&version=6.26.0) if you'd like.

title: Better docs for eslint
body:
Most people integrate prettier with eslint. We should put front and center how to plug in the config and plugin projects.

Help wanted.

title: Potentially include parentheses on expressions mixing math and binary operators
body:
Another case like https://github.com/prettier/prettier/issues/187 that I ran into in https://github.com/babel/babel/pull/5412

```js
const result = (a + b) >>> 1;
```
becomes
```js
const result = a + b >>> 1;
```

and I'd be very surprised if anyone remembers the precedence for binary math operators.
 
Thoughts?

title: handlebars attrbutes changed: {{@x.y.z}} becomes {{@x}}
body:
**Prettier 3.3.1** [Playground link](https://prettier.io/playground/#) ```sh --parser glimmer ``` **Input:** ```hbs

{{ title }}

{{@x.y.z}} {{ body }}
``` **Output:** ```hbs

{{title}}

{{@x}} {{body}}
``` **Expected behavior:** Starting with 3.3.1 {{@x.y.z}} is output as {{@x}} , if I add `--debug-check` it will catch that the output is different
